name: Joya Sherrill
abstract: Joya Sherrill (August 20, 1924 – June 28, 2010) was an American jazz vocalist and children's television show host.

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Mauve (The Blues)

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: And we had this woman singer, Joya Sherrill... I was standing right there, she says... Joya goes over to Benny
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...when he then went to Joya Sherrill and Tony Watkins... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Not Joya Sherill. [INTERVIEWEE] [knows Marl, but cannot recall the singer’s name.]
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Joya Sherill was there, and Louis Prima. Milt Grayson... Butch Ballard was there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And of course our principals, Lil Greenwood, Joya Sherrill, and Buddy Briggs.
